title: #undef should be preferred with caution
description: "#undef" should be used with cautionAnalyze your codeintentionality-clearmaintainabilityCode Smellbased-on-misrabrain-overloadWhy is this an issue?More InfoCode that contains many macros becomes hard to understand. This is even worse when the set of defined macros is not stable, and you have to know at each point what macros are defined. Therefore,#undefcan decrease the readability of macros.However, well-disciplined use of#undefcan also improve readability, for instance when defining a macro with a limited scope: The macro is#defined, used a couple of times to reduce code duplication, then immediately#undefed.This rule raises an issue when a#undefundefines a macro that was defined in another file.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: #undef should be preferred with caution. Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.
